summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI nightly, validates the roster response, and persists the structured JSON to a dedicated Azure Blob Storage account using Managed Identity authentication. The plan covers Bicep IaC provisioning, Python Azure Function implementation, CI/CD pipeline setup, security hardening, observability configuration, and full validation through staging slot swap before production release.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer token authentication; if not, Key Vault-stored credentials will be used via Key Vault references in App Settings.
The TRAPI endpoint base URL and API version will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-02 begins.
The roster scope is limited to the 25-man active roster (24–28 player validation range) unless otherwise confirmed.
TRAPI is accessible over the public internet; no VNet integration required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; no strict cold-start SLA on 2 AM UTC execution.
The existing repository structure (function_app.py, blob_writer.py, trapi_client.py) will be refactored to meet spec requirements rather than rewritten from scratch.
GitHub Actions workflows will deploy to an Azure staging slot before swapping to production.
Application Insights workspace-based instance will be used (not classic).
Python 3.11 runtime is confirmed for the Function App.
The dedicated data storage account is separate from the Azure Functions host storage account.
open_questions: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens? If not, what credential type is needed and where is it stored? (Owner: rciapala)
What is the TRAPI base URL, API version, and GPT-4o model deployment name to pin in the prompt template?
Should the roster include coaching staff, injured list, or front-office personnel in addition to the 25-man active roster?
Is TRAPI network-restricted, requiring VNet integration and private endpoints for storage?
Is there a strict SLA on the 2 AM UTC execution window that would require upgrading from Consumption to Premium Plan?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish infrastructure, secrets management, identity, CI/CD scaffolding, and resolve external blockers before any application code is merged.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and implementation plan approved
Azure subscription and resource group available
TRAPI endpoint and auth mechanism confirmed by rciapala
GitHub repository access confirmed for CI/CD setup
exit_criteria: Bicep IaC provisions all cloud resources successfully in a dev/staging environment
Managed Identity role assignments verified (Storage Blob Data Contributor, Key Vault Secrets User)
GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs and passes on pull requests
TRAPI connectivity and auth validated from a local or CI context
Plan approval task complete

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement all functional requirements: TRAPI integration with prompt template, response validation, blob write with idempotency, and nightly Timer Trigger.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint, auth scope, and model version confirmed
Bicep-provisioned infrastructure available for integration testing
exit_criteria: GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function executes end-to-end in staging
Validated roster written to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json
Failed responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
Conditional PUT prevents duplicate writes on retrigger
Timer Trigger fires at 0 0 2 * * * UTC with useMonitor: true
Unit tests pass with ≥80% coverage on core modules

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Implement reliability controls, observability instrumentation, security hardening, alerting rules, and data quality metrics.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Application Insights workspace provisioned and connected to Function App
Staging slot available for end-to-end testing
exit_criteria: Structured log events emitted for all key function lifecycle events
player_count_returned custom metric emitted on each run
All three alert rules active: execution failure, duration > 90s, data quality drift
Exponential backoff retry (3 retries, 45s timeout) verified under simulated TRAPI failure
No hardcoded secrets; all config via environment variables or Key Vault references
Integration test verifies Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in blob output

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Complete staging validation, production deployment via slot swap, documentation, and confirm all success criteria from spec are met.
exit_criteria: Production deployment completed via GitHub Actions staging slot swap
Monitoring and alerting active in production
README documents local dev setup, TRAPI auth instructions, and blob naming convention
All spec success criteria checked off

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Stakeholder review and sign-off on this implementation task plan before engineering work begins. Confirm open questions (TRAPI auth, endpoint, player scope, networking) are assigned to owners with deadlines.
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by rciapala and at least one engineer
All open questions assigned to owners with target resolution dates
Plan approved and engineering work authorized to begin
risks: TRAPI auth mechanism unresolved blocks APP-02 and INF-04
Player scope ambiguity affects prompt template and validation thresholds

tasks.title: Provision core Bicep IaC for Function App and host storage
tasks.description: Author or update Bicep modules under /infra to provision the Linux Consumption Plan Function App (Python 3.11) with system-assigned Managed Identity, the Azure Functions host storage account (separate from data storage), and all required App Settings including AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName for identity-based host storage.
acceptance_criteria: Function App provisions successfully via `az deployment group create`
System-assigned Managed Identity is enabled on the Function App
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName is set (no listKeys/shared key for host storage)
functionTimeout is 120 seconds in host.json
maxConcurrentCalls = 1 in host.json Timer Trigger config
Python 3.11 Linux Consumption Plan confirmed in Azure portal after deploy
risks: Consumption Plan cold-start may cause missed 2 AM executions if SLA is strict

tasks.title: Provision dedicated data storage account and blob container
tasks.description: Author or update /infra/modules/storage.bicep to provision a dedicated Standard_LRS Cool-tier storage account with allowSharedKeyAccess:false, a private yankees-roster container (no anonymous access), blob soft-delete with 7-day retention, and versioning enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Storage account is Standard_LRS, Cool access tier
allowSharedKeyAccess is false
Container yankees-roster has publicAccess: None
Blob soft-delete retention is 7 days
Blob versioning is enabled
Storage account is tagged project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala
risks: Cool tier adds latency for first-byte reads; acceptable for nightly write workload

tasks.title: Provision Key Vault and wire Key Vault references to Function App
tasks.description: Add /infra/modules/keyvault.bicep to provision a zone-redundant Azure Key Vault. Grant the Function App Managed Identity the Key Vault Secrets User role. Wire Key Vault references for TRAPI endpoint, auth scope, and any non-Managed-Identity credentials into Function App App Settings.
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned with zone-redundant SKU
Function App Managed Identity has Key Vault Secrets User role assignment
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE App Settings use @Microsoft.KeyVault(...) references
No plaintext secrets in Bicep or App Settings
Key Vault diagnostic logs forwarded to Application Insights workspace
risks: Key Vault reference resolution requires Managed Identity to be fully provisioned first
TRAPI credential type unknown until PROJ-01 open question is resolved

tasks.title: Provision Managed Identity RBAC role assignments
tasks.description: Author or update /infra/modules/rbac.bicep to grant the Function App Managed Identity Storage Blob Data Contributor on the yankees-roster container (container-scoped, not account-level) and Queue Data Contributor on the host storage account. Add TRAPI-specific role/scope assignment if required by TRAPI team.
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor assigned at container scope (not account scope)
Queue Data Contributor assigned on host storage account
Role assignments survive redeployment idempotently
No Storage Account Key-based auth; allowSharedKeyAccess remains false
RBAC assignment verified via `az role assignment list` post-deploy
risks: Container-scoped RBAC may require explicit resource ID path; verify Bicep resourceId syntax

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights workspace and alert rules
tasks.description: Update /infra/modules/monitoring.bicep to provision a workspace-based Application Insights instance with 30-day log retention and sampling enabled. Add /infra/modules/alerts.bicep with three alert rules: execution failure count > 0 in 1h, execution duration > 90s, and custom metric player_count_returned outside [24, 40].
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights workspace-based, 30-day retention, sampling enabled
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING wired into Function App App Settings
Execution failure alert fires for failure count > 0 in 1-hour window
Duration alert fires when execution duration > 90 seconds
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned < 24 or > 40
All alerts notify rciapala via email action group
risks: Custom metric player_count_returned alert requires metric to be emitted by app code before alert can be tested end-to-end

tasks.title: Validate TRAPI auth mechanism and document auth pattern
tasks.description: Confirm with the TRAPI team whether Managed Identity bearer tokens are supported for GPT-4o access. Document the required auth scope, credential type, and storage location. Update Key Vault secrets and Function App App Settings accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI auth mechanism confirmed in writing from TRAPI team
TRAPI_ENDPOINT secret stored in Key Vault
TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE or API key stored in Key Vault (as applicable)
trapi_client.py updated to use confirmed auth pattern
No TRAPI credentials appear in source code or App Settings plaintext
risks: If Managed Identity is unsupported, API key rotation process must be defined
TRAPI team availability may block this task and delay APP-02

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline
tasks.description: Create or update .github/workflows/ci.yml to run on pull requests: install dependencies from requirements.txt, run pytest with coverage, and enforce a minimum coverage threshold. Pipeline must pass before merge to main.
acceptance_criteria: CI pipeline triggers on pull_request to main
pytest runs with --cov and enforces ≥80% coverage threshold
Pipeline fails fast on test failure or coverage miss
CI badge added to README
risks: Existing test coverage may be below threshold; baseline must be measured before enforcing

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CD pipeline with staging slot swap
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd.yml triggered on push to main. Pipeline deploys to a staging slot, runs a smoke test (verify latest blob exists or function health endpoint responds), then performs a production slot swap. Uses OIDC Federated Identity for Azure authentication (no stored secrets).
acceptance_criteria: CD pipeline deploys to staging slot on push to main
Smoke test verifies function app is responsive before slot swap
Slot swap to production only occurs after smoke test passes
Pipeline uses OIDC (no stored Azure credentials in GitHub Secrets)
Failed smoke test prevents slot swap and raises pipeline failure notification
risks: Staging slot on Consumption Plan may have cold-start delay affecting smoke test timing

tasks.title: Author prompt template and pin GPT-4o model version
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with a clear, deterministic prompt requesting the 1985 NY Yankees roster in the required JSON schema. Pin the GPT-4o model deployment version in configuration. Include prompt hash logging support.
inputs: Confirmed TRAPI model deployment name from SEC-01
outputs: GPT4O_MODEL_VERSION config key documented in local.settings.json.example
acceptance_criteria: Prompt template stored at prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt in source control
Prompt explicitly requests JSON output matching the players schema
GPT-4o model deployment version is pinned in App Settings (not hardcoded in code)
Prompt file is loaded at runtime (not embedded as string literal)
Prompt hash is computable for log emission in trapi_request_sent event
risks: Model version availability on TRAPI may differ from public GPT-4o versions

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with retry, timeout, and auth
tasks.description: Refactor or implement trapi_client.py to: acquire a Managed Identity bearer token for the confirmed TRAPI auth scope, POST the prompt to the TRAPI GPT-4o endpoint, enforce a 45-second HTTP timeout, and implement 3-retry exponential backoff on transient failures (5xx, timeout). Emit structured log events trapi_request_sent and trapi_response_received.
acceptance_criteria: Bearer token acquired via DefaultAzureCredential with confirmed TRAPI scope
HTTP POST to TRAPI with 45-second timeout enforced
3 retries with exponential backoff on 5xx and timeout errors
trapi_request_sent log event includes model version and prompt hash
trapi_response_received log event includes token count, latency ms, and player count
Unit tests mock TRAPI HTTP calls and verify retry behavior
No TRAPI endpoint or credentials hardcoded in source
risks: TRAPI rate limits or quota constraints may cause retries to exhaust before success

tasks.title: Implement roster response validation
tasks.description: Implement a validation module that checks the TRAPI response against the expected JSON schema (players array with name, position, jersey_number fields) and validates player count is in the range [24, 28]. On validation failure, return the raw response for failed-blob routing. On success, return the parsed roster.
acceptance_criteria: Schema validation rejects responses missing name, position, or jersey_number fields
Player count outside [24, 28] triggers validation failure
Validation passes for well-formed responses with 24–28 players
Unit tests cover: valid response, missing fields, count too low, count too high
Known player assertion available as utility (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present)
risks: GPT-4o output may occasionally include coaching staff inflating player count above 28

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and failed-blob routing
tasks.description: Refactor blob_writer.py to: write validated roster to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using DefaultAzureCredential, enforce conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) to prevent duplicate writes on retrigger, and write failed/invalid responses to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json. Emit blob_write_succeeded log event with blob URI.
inputs: INF-02 storage account name and container
acceptance_criteria: BlobServiceClient uses DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings or shared keys)
Blob name is {run_date_utc}.json in UTC
Conditional PUT with If-None-Match: * raises ResourceExistsError on duplicate write
Duplicate write is logged as warning and function exits gracefully (not error)
Failed validation response written to failed/{run_date_utc}.json
blob_write_succeeded log event includes full blob URI
Unit tests verify conditional PUT, duplicate handling, and failed-blob routing
risks: Cool tier storage may add write latency; measure against 60-second completion target

tasks.title: Implement Timer Trigger function orchestrator
tasks.description: Implement or update function_app.py with the GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger function. Configure schedule 0 0 2 * * * UTC, useMonitor: true, and maxConcurrentCalls: 1. Orchestrate: load prompt, call TRAPI client, validate response, write blob (success or failed path), emit all structured log events, and emit player_count_returned custom metric.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule is 0 0 2 * * * with explicit UTC timezone
useMonitor: true set in trigger configuration
maxConcurrentCalls = 1 in host.json
functionTimeout = 120 in host.json
All five structured log events emitted: function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed
player_count_returned custom metric emitted via Application Insights SDK on each successful run
Function exits with non-zero code on unhandled exception to trigger failure alert
risks: OpenTelemetry SDK integration with Azure Functions v2 may require additional configuration

tasks.title: Write unit tests for all core application modules
tasks.description: Ensure comprehensive unit test coverage for trapi_client.py, validator.py, blob_writer.py, and function_app.py. Tests must mock all external dependencies (HTTP, Azure SDK). Cover happy path, retry exhaustion, validation failure, duplicate blob, and failed-blob routing scenarios.
acceptance_criteria: pytest --cov reports ≥80% coverage across all core modules
TRAPI retry logic tested: 3 retries, exponential backoff, final failure behavior
Validator tests: valid schema, missing fields, count out of range
Blob writer tests: successful write, conditional PUT conflict, failed blob path
Function orchestrator test: end-to-end mock with known player assertions (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson)
All tests pass in CI pipeline
risks: Mocking DefaultAzureCredential and Azure SDK clients requires careful fixture design

tasks.title: Instrument structured logging and custom metrics via OpenTelemetry
tasks.description: Integrate the OpenTelemetry SDK with the Azure Functions v2 app. Emit all required structured log events with appropriate fields. Emit the player_count_returned custom metric to Application Insights on each successful run. Verify log events appear in Application Insights Live Metrics and query results.
acceptance_criteria: All five log events appear in Application Insights customEvents or traces table
player_count_returned appears in Application Insights customMetrics table
Log events include required fields (model version, prompt hash, token count, latency, player count, blob URI)
Sampling is enabled and does not drop > 10% of events at 1 execution/day volume
OpenTelemetry SDK dependencies added to requirements-prod.txt
Log retention confirmed at 30 days in Log Analytics workspace
risks: Azure Functions v2 Python model may require specific OpenTelemetry configuration to avoid duplicate telemetry

tasks.title: Security hardening: secrets audit and least-privilege review
tasks.description: Audit all App Settings, source code, and Bicep templates for hardcoded secrets or over-permissive RBAC assignments. Verify Key Vault references resolve correctly. Confirm Storage Blob Data Contributor is scoped to container (not account). Ensure no anonymous blob access.
acceptance_criteria: No secrets in source code (grep for connection strings, API keys, passwords returns no hits)
All sensitive App Settings use @Microsoft.KeyVault(...) references
Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to container, not storage account
yankees-roster container publicAccess is None (verified via Azure portal or CLI)
Key Vault access policy / RBAC shows only Function App Managed Identity has Secrets User
allowSharedKeyAccess is false on data storage account
risks: Key Vault reference resolution failures cause Function App startup failure; test in staging first

tasks.title: End-to-end staging environment validation
tasks.description: Manually trigger the GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function in the staging slot. Verify the complete flow: TRAPI call succeeds, roster is validated, blob is written to staging storage account, Application Insights logs all events, custom metric is emitted, and all alert rules are reachable.
inputs: Staging slot deployed via CD pipeline
outputs: Staging validation sign-off document (PR comment or issue comment)
acceptance_criteria: Function executes end-to-end in staging without errors
yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json blob exists in staging storage container
Blob contains valid JSON with players array including Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson
Application Insights shows all five structured log events for the run
player_count_returned metric appears in customMetrics table
Conditional PUT: re-triggering on same day produces no duplicate blob and logs warning
Simulated TRAPI failure writes to failed/ prefix and alert fires
risks: TRAPI staging environment may differ from production; confirm endpoint availability

tasks.title: Write integration test for known player assertion
tasks.description: Create /tests/integration/test_integration.py that runs against a live or recorded TRAPI response and verifies the blob output contains the three known 1985 Yankees players: Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson. Can be run in CI against a mock or in staging against live TRAPI.
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, Rickey Henderson present in response
Test verifies player count is in [24, 28]
Test can run in CI with recorded/fixture response (no live TRAPI dependency)
Test can be optionally run against live staging TRAPI with environment flag
Test passes in CI pipeline
risks: GPT-4o response non-determinism may cause occasional false failures; use recorded fixtures for CI

tasks.title: Author README with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming
tasks.description: Update readme.md with: local development setup instructions (Python venv, requirements.txt, local.settings.json.example usage), TRAPI authentication instructions (how to obtain and configure credentials), blob naming convention explanation, and links to Application Insights workspace and Key Vault.
acceptance_criteria: README covers local dev setup: Python 3.11, venv creation, pip install -r requirements.txt
README explains TRAPI auth: how to obtain credentials and configure local.settings.json
README documents blob naming convention: yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json
README includes failed blob path: yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
README includes link to Application Insights workspace (or instructions to find it)
README includes CI badge and deployment instructions
risks: TRAPI auth instructions depend on SEC-01 resolution; doc cannot be finalized until auth is confirmed

tasks.title: Production deployment via GitHub Actions slot swap
tasks.description: Execute the CD pipeline on main branch to deploy to staging slot, run smoke test, and swap to production. Verify production Function App is healthy, monitoring is active, and first scheduled execution at 2 AM UTC is observed in Application Insights.
outputs: First 2 AM UTC execution log in Application Insights
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions CD pipeline completes without errors
Production slot swap verified in Azure portal
All three Application Insights alert rules are active in production
Function App health check responds in production
First 2 AM UTC execution observed in Application Insights with all five log events
player_count_returned metric emitted in production customMetrics
No secrets in environment; all Key Vault references resolve
risks: Consumption Plan cold start at first 2 AM execution may cause a timeout; monitor first run
Slot swap may cause brief downtime; acceptable for nightly non-SLA workload
